entries.title: Zelensky says failure of US envoys to visit Kyiv is 'disrespectful'
entries.snippet: US envoy Steve Witkoff and Donald Trump's son-in-law Jared Kushner have travelled to Moscow several times, but never to Kyiv.

entries.title: Unprecedented ruling finds Hungary's anti-LGBTQ laws in breach of EU values
entries.snippet: The EU's top court finds that the reforms breached EU values on a number of levels and broke the founding values of the EU treaty.

entries.title: Japan loosens arms export rules in break from post-WW2 pacifism
entries.snippet: This clears the way for Japan to sell weapons to more than a dozen countries.

entries.title: South Africa's police boss charged in connection with controversial health contract
entries.snippet: Fannie Masemola is accused of failing in his duties to provide proper oversight in his role.

entries.title: Role of US officials killed in crash in Mexico under scrutiny
entries.snippet: The two died in a car crash after a Mexican-led operation to destroy a clandestine drug lab.

entries.title: Madonna offers reward for return of missing Coachella costume
entries.snippet: The items, which she says are "part of my history", disappeared after her slot with Sabrina Carpenter.

entries.title: Arrests in Nigeria after abduction of local monarch 'held for ransom'
entries.snippet: The kidnappers raided his palace on Saturday and are believed to be holding him in a forest.

entries.title: Canadian killed in shooting at Mexico's ancient Teotihuacán pyramids
entries.snippet: Several people were also injured in the incident on Monday at the popular tourist site, say Mexican officials, weeks before the country hosts the Fifa World Cup.

entries.title: US singer D4vd pleads not guilty to murder in death of missing teen girl
entries.snippet: Prosecutors say the singer, real name David Anthony Burke, could face life in prison due to the nature of the case, if convicted.

entries.title: The Onion launches new effort to turn Infowars into parody website
entries.snippet: The new proposal would involve the publication licensing Alex Jones's company, which faces liquidation.

entries.title: Ofcom investigating Telegram over child sexual abuse material concerns
entries.snippet: The popular messaging service told the BBC in a statement it "categorically denies Ofcom's accusations".

entries.title: Outrage over Israeli soldier's vandalism of Jesus statue in Lebanon
entries.snippet: The Israeli military says it views with "great severity" the actions of the soldier, as the US ambassador demands "swift" consequences.

entries.title: Japan on high alert for 'huge' second quake after lifting tsunami warning
entries.snippet: Japan's meteorological agency warns another, stronger earthquake may hit in the next week.

entries.title: Blue Origin rocket grounded after satellite 'mishap'
entries.snippet: The firm founded by Amazon billionaire Jeff Bezos is investigating the failed launch.

entries.title: Police gunfight with favela gang traps 200 tourists on hilltop
entries.snippet: The access route to Morro Dois Irmãos was blocked during the operation in Vidigal, leaving scores of alarmed sightseers stuck on the hill.

entries.title: How will Apple change under 'product guy' John Ternus?
entries.snippet: As Tim Cook announces his departure from the top job at Apple, these are the key challenges for new boss John Ternus.

entries.title: Indian politicians are campaigning while holding fish. What is going on?
entries.snippet: In West Bengal polls, fish moves from plate to politics, symbolising identity and cultural pride.

entries.title: Nigerian wins global prize for trying to save bats in a country that shuns them
entries.snippet: Many Nigerians associate bats with witchcraft but that did not deter ecologist Iroro Tanshi.

entries.title: The insider trading suspicions looming over Trump's presidency
entries.snippet: The BBC has found a pattern of spikes in trades ahead of public announcements by the US president.

entries.title: 'They told me he was dead': Children born near army base learn truth about UK soldier dads
entries.snippet: A DNA and legal project has identified the fathers of 20 children born near a military base in Kenya.

entries.title: As the U.S.-Iran ceasefire deadline looms, here are the main sticking points
entries.snippet: The status of the Strait of Hormuz and Iran's nuclear enrichment program are among the biggest obstacles to extending the truce, which expires Wednesday evening.

entries.title: U.S.-Iran ceasefire nears its end. And, Fed chair nominee faces tough hearing
entries.snippet: The ceasefire between the U.S. and Iran is set to expire tomorrow. Peace talks between the countries remain uncertain. And, Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve faces a tough confirmation hearing today.

entries.title: The hidden power keeping wages low
entries.snippet: For decades, economists gave short shrift to the idea of monopsony — a power employers can have to suppress wages. Now a wave of research suggests it's everywhere, and a new book argues it's key to understanding today's inequality.

entries.title: She raised concerns about her company's contracts with ICE. Then she lost her job
entries.snippet: Billie Little had worked for Thomson Reuters for about two decades. She was fired after questioning whether federal immigration agents unlawfully used their products.

entries.title: The surprising origin of 4 features that superglue kids — and adults — to screens
entries.snippet: Taken together, these four features can create a trancelike state that can keep us stuck on social media apps or video games for hours. Children are particularly vulnerable.

entries.title: 3 things to know about Fed chair nominee Kevin Warsh
entries.snippet: President Trump's pick to lead the Federal Reserve goes before a Senate committee today — but Kevin Warsh's confirmation could be held up by forces that are outside his control.

entries.title: Gunman shoots several tourists at historic pyramids in Mexico
entries.snippet: A man standing atop one of the historic Teotihuacan pyramids opened fire on tourists Monday, killing one Canadian and leaving at least 13 people, authorities said.

entries.title: Cuba confirms meeting with US officials on island, wants energy blockade lifted
entries.snippet: Cuba's government confirmed that it had recently met with U.S. officials on the island as tensions between the two sides remain high over the U.S. energy blockade of the Caribbean country.

entries.title: Japan approves scrapping a ban on lethal weapons exports
entries.snippet: The approval clears a final set of hurdles for Japan's postwar arms sales and facilitate its future sale of weapons such as a next-generation fighter jet and combat drones.

entries.title: Here are the results for Virginia's 2026 redistricting ballot measure
entries.snippet: Live election results: Get the latest on Virginia's special election on redistricting.

entries.title: Next round of U.S.-Iran talks in doubt as tensions rise ahead of ceasefire deadline
entries.snippet: An American delegation, led by Vice President Vance, will soon leave for Pakistan to resume talks with Iran over the nearly two-month-long war. Iranian officials have been noncommittal about attending the round of talks. President Trump also sent mixed signals, predicting success

entries.title: Retired admiral breaks down U.S. strategy behind naval blockade
entries.snippet: To discuss the U.S. shooting and disabling of an Iranian cargo ship to enforce its naval blockade, Geoff Bennett spoke with retired Adm. Gary Roughead. He was Chief of Naval Operations from 2007 to 2011 and is now a Distinguished Military Fellow at the Hoover Institution at Stanf

entries.title: Middle East experts analyze U.S. and Iran priorities in potential negotiations
entries.snippet: For additional perspective on the state of diplomacy with Iran, Amna Nawaz spoke with Alan Eyre and Miad Maleki. Eyre was part of the Obama administration's negotiating team for the Iran nuclear deal and is now at the Middle East Institute. Maleki was born and raised in Iran and

entries.title: News Wrap: Patel sues The Atlantic over story alleging excessive drinking and absences
entries.snippet: In our news wrap Monday, FBI Director Kash Patel filed a lawsuit against The Atlantic magazine over a story alleging "excessive drinking and unexplained absences," Monday marked the first day businesses could submit refund requests for tariffs paid before they were ruled unconsti

entries.title: Killing of 8 children in Louisiana renews focus on domestic violence involving guns
entries.snippet: Another deadly shooting has torn apart another American community. In Shreveport, Louisiana, a man shot and killed eight children and left two other adults gravely wounded. The killer was reportedly the father of seven of the eight children he shot. William Brangham discussed the

entries.title: Redistricting battle reaches Virginia as voters weigh new congressional map
entries.snippet: In Virginia, voters are heading to the polls on Tuesday for a high-stakes ballot measure that could reshape the state's congressional map and potentially shift the balance of power in Washington. Supporters say it's a necessary response to aggressive Republican-led redistricting

entries.title: Tamara Keith and Amy Walter on partisan redistricting and the midterms
entries.snippet: NPR's Tamara Keith and Amy Walter of the Cook Political Report with Amy Walter join Geoff Bennett to discuss the latest political news, including Labor Secretary Lori Chavez-DeRemer leaving her post, partisan redistricting and the midterms and Democratic fundraising advantages.

entries.title: Cities turn streetlights red to protect nocturnal ecosystems
entries.snippet: International Dark Sky Week is hailed as a global celebration of the night and a call for less light pollution. In some cities internationally, local governments are trying to find a solution to lighting the night can better serve critical wildlife ecosystems and humans alike. Ma

entries.title: Lori Chavez-DeRemer is out as labor secretary, White House says
entries.snippet: Chavez-DeRemer is the third Trump Cabinet member to leave her post in recent months.

entries.title: Apple CEO Tim Cook to step down, ending nearly 15-year reign leading the iPhone maker
entries.snippet: Cook will turn the CEO duties to Apple's head of hardware engineering, John Tenus, on Sept. 1 while remaining involved with the company as executive chairman.

entries.title: Four U.N. secretary-general candidates audition this week. That's far fewer than in 2016
entries.snippet: Chile's former President Michelle Bachelet — one of two women and one of three from Latin America — will be the first to face ambassadors from the U.N.'s 193 member nations during a three-hour question-and-answer session Tuesday.

entries.title: Mexico's Sheinbaum demands explanation after U.S. officials die assisting in operation in Chihuahua
entries.snippet: Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum says she was unaware of any collaboration between the U.S. and local authorities in Chihuahua after four officials died in an accident over the weekend.

entries.title: Gunman at Mexico's Teotihuacán pyramids kills 1 Canadian tourist, injures 6
entries.snippet: A man with a gun opened fire Monday at the historic Teotihuacán pyramids, killing one Canadian tourist and injuring six other people at the tourist site an hour north of Mexico's capital, the Mexican government said.

entries.title: A long-shuttered Iraq-Syria border crossing reopens for the first time in more than a decade
entries.snippet: A key border crossing between Iraq and Syria reopened on Monday for the first time in more than a decade, with officials highlighting its potential for trade and oil exports.

entries.title: AP sources: Witnesses subpoenaed to testify before D.C. grand jury in John Brennan investigation
entries.snippet: The Justice Department has subpoenaed several witnesses to testify before a federal grand jury in Washington as part of its investigation into former CIA Director John Brennan.

entries.title: Actors reenact Paul Revere's historic midnight ride with some modern-day tweaks
entries.snippet: Revere's ride took place on April 18, 1775, when the silversmith and express rider was dispatched to Lexington to warn Revolutionary leaders Samuel Adams and John Hancock that the British were coming to arrest them.

entries.title: FBI Director Kash Patel sues The Atlantic for article that alleged excessive drinking
entries.snippet: FBI Director Kash Patel has filed a $250 million lawsuit against The Atlantic magazine and its reporter Sarah Fitzpatrick, contending its article posted last week that talked about his alleged excessive drinking was a "malicious hit piece."

entries.title: Migrants rush to apply under Spain's new mass legalization program
entries.snippet: Spain has launched a mass legalization measure for migrants, allowing them to apply in-person for legal status. It contrasts with other European countries that are tightening immigration controls.

entries.title: Oil prices rise and stocks give back part of record-breaking rally following latest Iran tensions
entries.snippet: Oil prices are climbing following the latest rise of U.S.-Iran tensions, but the moves are more modest than they were earlier in the war.

entries.title: Singer D4vd charged with murder of 14-year-old found decomposed in his car
entries.snippet: Los Angeles prosecutors said Monday that the 21-year-old singer whose legal name is David Burke was charged with first-degree murder in the killing of Celeste Rivas Hernandez.

entries.title: DW
entries.snippet: News, Analysis and Service from Germany and Europe - in 30 Languages

entries.title: Apple switches CEO: What it means for the iPhone maker
entries.snippet: Apple's top hardware engineer John Ternus will lead the $4 trillion iPhone maker, as Tim Cook steps back. Will Apple chase rivals for the smartest intelligence, or deliver the best personal AI experience?

entries.title: Hungary: Orban-era LGBTQ law infringes human rights, ECJ rules
entries.snippet: The EU's top court has found that Hungary's 2021 laws on issues including transgender and homosexual identity and sex offenses breach various EU laws. This includes Article 2 of EU Treaty, to protect human dignity.

entries.title: Why the cloud still runs on coal and gas
entries.snippet: Renewable energy is increasingly being used to supply power-hungry data centers. As the sector swells, much of the electricity demand is being met by polluting fossil fuels.

entries.title: Japan loosens long-standing curbs on arms exports
entries.snippet: Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi approved new rules expanding Japan's ability to export advanced defense equipment, a major shift as Tokyo boosts defense spending and its arms industry.

entries.title: Germany news: Trial starts in Istanbul over tourist deaths
entries.snippet: Six people have been charged with negligent homicide over the deaths of four members of a German family at a hotel in Turkey. Meanwhile, a major climate conference is starting in Berlin. DW has more.

entries.title: India news: India-US near trade deal in Washington
entries.snippet: US officials say an agreement is nearing completion as negotiations resume. Meanwhile, videos of violence inside a German gurudwara are causing a stir in India. Follow live.

entries.title: South Korea: Police seek to arrest BTS agency chief over fraud allegations
entries.snippet: Police say HYBE chief Bang Si-hyuk secretly made millions from post-IPO share sales. The case comes as K-pop supergroup BTS embarks on a global tour.

entries.title: Rat poison found in baby food, extortion suspected
entries.snippet: Rat poison has been found in HiPP baby food jars across Austria, Czechia and Slovakia in what authorities suspect is a blackmail attempt.

entries.title: Iran war: Talks in doubt as leaders issue threats
entries.snippet: While the talks remain in limbo, the United States and Iran have been trading threats. Separately, media reports suggest US Vice President JD Vance is set to travel to Pakistan for ceasefire negotiations. DW has more.

entries.title: FAA grounds Blue Origin's New Glenn after orbital setback
entries.snippet: US aviation regulators have ordered Blue Origin, a space company owned by billionaire Jeff Bezos, to investigate a malfunction in the upper stage of its New Glenn rocket.

entries.title: Amnesty International paints a grim picture for human rights
entries.snippet: In its 2025/2026 report, the human rights watchdog Amnesty International calls out the "predatory behavior of the powerful" and calls for action to defend the global order.

entries.title: Tim Cook to step down as Apple CEO, John Ternus to take over
entries.snippet: John Ternus, who has long focused ​on design and ‌products at Apple, will lead the iPhone maker as it gears up for an industry change spurred by artificial intelligence.

entries.title: Mexico: Canadian killed in shooting at Teotihuacan pyramid
entries.snippet: A gunman opened fire at the popular tourist and archeological site outside Mexico city.

entries.title: UK: Starmer says he would't have appointed Mandelson as US envoy if he'd known he failed vetting process
entries.snippet: Under-fire Prime Minister Keir Starmer told the House of Commons that neither he nor his ministers were told that the former ambassador to the US and Epstein associate Peter Mandelson had failed his security vetting.

entries.title: Elon Musk skips probe as France investigates X
entries.snippet: French prosecutors are investigating whether content generated by X's AI chatbot Grok contributed to the spread of child sexual abuse material, explicit deepfakes and Holocaust denial.

entries.title: Ugandan military reports rescue of some 200 hostages from ADF militants in eastern DRC
entries.snippet: Uganda's military has said that it and forces from the neighboring Democratic Republic of Congo freed some 200 hostages in eastern DRC in a joint operation against Islamist militants known as the ADF.

entries.title: Unpacking the 2026 Venice Biennale controversies and highlights
entries.snippet: Russia's return to the international contemporary art exhibition, as well as the participation of Israel and the US, have sparked politicized backlash.

entries.title: Sudan war fuels child hunger crisis
entries.snippet: Sudan's war, now in its fourth year, has displaced millions, leaving parts of the country facing famine. Aid agencies warn that children are bearing the brunt as food shortages worsen and humanitarian funding declines.

entries.title: Is Vietnam becoming more like China?
entries.snippet: After Vietnam's lawmakers unanimously elected Communist Party chief To Lam as the country's president, analysts are asking whether Vietnam aims to copy Chinese President Xi Jinping's political model.
